front: Work: District Officer Simon Pilkings tries to stop Elesin Oba's ritual suicide following the king's death
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: the protagonist tells the Praise-Singer the story of the 'Not-I Bird' to prove his readiness to die
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Olunde, a medical student, commits suicide instead of his father; Iyaloja reveals his body
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Iyaloja, 'mother of the market,' allows the protagonist to marry her son's fiancee before the ritual
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: the protagonist strangles himself with his prison chains as his unnamed Bride watches
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: the author's note attacks the 'facile tag' of 'clash of cultures' and calls the 'Colonial Factor' merely a 'catalytic incident'
back: Death and the King's Horseman (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Baroka ('the Lion') feigns impotence to seduce Sidi ('the Jewel') away from the Westernized teacher Lakunle
back: The Lion and the Jewel (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Lakunle refuses to pay the bride-price and calls the woman 'Ruth, Rachel, Esther, and Bathsheba'
back: The Lion and the Jewel (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Lakunle plays a photographer during the 'Dance of the Lost Traveller' in this play
back: The Lion and the Jewel (Wole Soyinka)

front: Work: Dr. Bero imprisons and shoots his cannibalistic father (The Old Man); four beggars worship the god 'As'
Work: Eman replaces a mute boy as the village 'carrier' (scapegoat) and is killed by Jaguna's trap
Author: this author's Nobel lecture 'This Past Must Address Its Present' discussed Mandela and criticized Frobenius's racist attribution of Yoruba art
Author: in this poet's 'Telephone Conversation,' a landlady asks 'ARE YOU DARK? OR VERY LIGHT?' in all caps
Author: this author translated D.O. Fagunwa's Forest of a Thousand Daemons into English
Author: this author was imprisoned for 22 months during the Nigerian Civil War, writing his memoir The Man Died on toilet paper
Author: this author adapted Euripides' Bacchae with a chorus of slaves, subtitled 'A Communion Rite'
Author: dictator President Kongi demands Oba Danlola's yams; Segi presents her father's head at the Festival of the New Yam
Author: the prostitute Rola learns she was a queen in her past life in this official play of Nigerian independence celebrations
back: Wole Soyinka [A Dance of the Forests]

front: Author: this author's essay on the 'Fourth Stage' linked a restorative deity and Nietzsche's Apollonian-Dionysian dichotomy to tragedy
Author: this author's 2021 novel Chronicles from the Land of the Happiest People on Earth was his first novel in 48 years; features Dr. Menka and selling human body parts
Author: this author satirized Idi Amin as the tyrannical Dr. Kamini in A Play of Giants
